mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding real economic output and higher corporate earnings, which directly supports equity prices over the next month. This is a persistent trend-following effect that works in any macro regime.
failure_condition: If the directional accuracy of a binary positive/negative month-over-month INDPRO change on 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly above 50% over any 3-year rolling window (p > 0.10 in one-sided binomial test).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, it signals extreme labor market distress that triggers aggressive policy easing and fiscal stimulus expectations, leading markets to price in a recovery and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return during UNRATE > 9% periods is not statistically significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p < 0.05) relative to zero or to a baseline of all periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are below 600 thousand units and the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25%, the housing market is at a cyclical trough with maximum monetary accommodation. This joint condition forecasts a strong rebound in equity prices over the next quarter as the housing cycle turns.
regime_hint: extreme policy accommodation and housing trough
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not yield positive 60-day forward returns in at least 70% of occurrences in walk-forward testing over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime where CPI year-over-year is below 2%, a positive month-over-month change in CPI signals that deflation risk is receding while interest rates remain low, reducing the real discount rate and supporting equity valuations.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPI YoY < 2% and a positive monthly CPI change, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p < 0.05) relative to zero.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate persistently below 0.5% indicates maximum monetary accommodation that depresses discount rates and encourages risk-taking, providing a sustained tailwind for equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the median 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% is not significantly different from zero (sign test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending which directly improves corporate revenues and earnings, leading to immediate positive equity returns over the short term.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly change in RSAFS and forward 5-day S&P 500 returns is not positive (Pearson r > 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the IC is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is above 8% but industrial production is rising month-over-month, it indicates that the industrial sector is recovering while the labor market remains slack—a classic early-cycle configuration that strongly signals positive equity returns over the next month.
regime_hint: early recovery with lagging labor market
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces a negative average 20-day forward return or fails to outperform a random periodic baseline (permutation test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a recession regime identified by two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP (GDPC1), a falling unemployment rate is a reliable signal that the economy is exiting recession, prompting forward-looking equity markets to rally over the next quarter.
regime_hint: NBER-style recession regime (GDPC1 sequentially declining for two or more quarters)
failure_condition: If during recession periods identified by the regime condition, the signal of declining UNRATE yields positive 60-day forward returns in less than 60% of instances or the mean return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
